judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with substantively equivalent findings and fixes; Report 2 additionally ties findings to explicit project-doc guardrails, but both are comparable in coverage of the stated expectations.

report_1.evidence: catches every primary-vendor exception with a broad except ... BadRequestError ... this indicates the request itself was malformed... It should not be silently retried... ContentPolicyError — this is the most concerning case... This should not be auto-retried at all; it should be surfaced/escalated... AuthError — this indicates a credentials/configuration failure on primary, which is not transient... Narrow the except to the retryable set (RateLimitError, RequestTimeoutError, ServerError) for the automatic-fallback path. Handle BadRequestError separately... Handle ContentPolicyError separately — do not auto-forward to a second vendor; quarantine/escalate instead. Handle AuthError with a fail-fast/circuit-breaker

report_2.evidence: replace the blanket except Exception with a narrow, explicit denylist of non-retryable exceptions that must never fail over, and let everything else retry: ... NON_RETRYABLE = (BadRequestError, ContentPolicyError, AuthError) ... except NON_RETRYABLE: raise

report_1.evidence: Path A — non-production bypass: ... returns True for any value other than the exact string "production", including an unset STAGE... Path C — production, RedisError: returns True (fail-open)... on RedisError, fail closed (return False)... make the stage check robust against naming drift

report_2.evidence: Triggers whenever STAGE is unset (None), or set to anything other than the exact string "production"... the limiter is fully bypassed. Inappropriate... On any Redis-layer failure... the limiter fails open, allowing unlimited calls until Redis recovers. Inappropriate... Fix: except RedisError: return False.

report_1.evidence: Do not log input_text at all — if a debugging signal is needed, log a length or a non-reversible fingerprint, not content. Replace the blanket metadata dump with an explicit allowlist of non-sensitive fields

report_2.evidence: input_text is logged verbatim and in full... metadata is logged with no allowlist at all... Concrete fix: ... "input_text_sha256": hashlib.sha256(record.input_text.encode()).hexdigest(), "input_text_len": len(record.input_text), "metadata": {k: v for k, v in record.metadata.items() if k in _SAFE_METADATA_KEYS}

report_1.evidence: Fail loudly if PII_SALT is missing or empty (raise a configuration error at startup rather than defaulting to "")

report_2.evidence: fail hard when the salt is missing instead of defaulting to "": ... if not salt: raise RuntimeError("PII_SALT is not configured; refusing to pseudonymize applicant id")
